name: example-search-indexing-feature
initialPrompt: We need to add full-text search to the docs site. Users keep complaining they can't find anything. I want: a search index built at deploy time, a search box in the header that does instant client-side filtering, and keyboard nav (arrow keys + enter) for the results dropdown. Also figure out whether we host the index ourselves or use a service — I don't want a monthly bill if we can avoid it.

cannedAuthorResponse.title: Full-text search for the docs site

blocks.text: Make docs content findable via in-page full-text search

blocks.question: Self-host the index or use a hosted search service?
blocks.rationale: Avoids recurring cost; index is small.

blocks.title: Build search index at deploy time
Add header search box with instant client-side filtering

blocks.description: Index could grow large enough to hurt first-load
blocks.mitigation: lazy-load index on first search interaction

blocks.question: Do we need stemming/fuzzy matching for v1, or is prefix match enough?

cannedForcedReviseResponse.title: Full-text search for the docs site

blocks.text: Make docs content findable via in-page full-text search with sub-100ms queries

blocks.title: Spike: measure real index size on current docs corpus
acceptance: index size for full corpus reported

blocks.question: Self-host the index or use a hosted search service?
blocks.rationale: Avoids recurring cost; spike confirms index stays small.

blocks.title: Build search index at deploy time
Add header search box with instant client-side filtering
Add '/' keyboard shortcut to focus the search box

blocks.description: Index could grow large enough to hurt first-load
blocks.mitigation: lazy-load index on first search interaction

blocks.question: Do we need stemming/fuzzy matching for v1, or is prefix match enough?

idChangedButCorresponding.note: Same intent (stemming/fuzzy vs prefix match) but the agent renumbered the open-question id during revise. Its ID legitimately changed, so it is NOT in expectedPreservedIds; the AC-13 re-anchoring fallback must map a comment on oq-stemming to oq-fuzzy-v2 via kind-gated Jaccard.

decoy.note: Genuinely-new block. Shares the surface tokens 'keyboard' and 'search box' with task-kbd-nav, making it a real trap for the re-anchoring fallback. A comment originally on task-kbd-nav must re-attach to task-kbd-nav (which kept its id), NOT to this decoy.

notes: Renumbering pressure is real: a spike task is inserted near the top (positional shift), a second new keyboard task is inserted, and the open-question id is renumbered. A naive positional ID scheme would lose obj/dec/task-build alignment after the inserted spike. expectedPreservedIds deliberately EXCLUDES oq-stemming (its id changed) and the two new tasks (genuinely new), so the AC-12 denominator is 6 and the fixture does not trivially preserve all ids.
